_warning: GENERATED FILE — do not hand-edit. Edit data/template-manifests/*.manifest.json and re-run `npx tsx src/scripts/buildTemplateManifests.ts`.

templates.description: A filled area of an aggregated measure over a truncated date; a color dimension splits the area into bands.

slots.notes: XML instance [tqr:Order Date:qk] derivation='Quarter-Trunc'; short-form 'tqr' IS a derivationMap key (long form differs: 'TruncQuarter'). Kept verbatim.
template column has aggregate-role-from='[State/Province]'; a cosmetic secondary-role hint carried onto the target, not rewritten (verification-report correction: GREEN -> YELLOW).

hazards.detail: [Region] aggregate-role-from='[State/Province]' is not rewritten by replaceFieldReferences; the target field inherits the template's secondary-role hint. Verification report corrects this template from GREEN to YELLOW. Safe under binding (cosmetic), so fast_path_eligible stays true.

templates.description: Stacked filled areas of an aggregated measure over a truncated date; a color dimension stacks the bands.

slots.notes: XML instance [tqr:Order Date:qk] derivation='Quarter-Trunc'; short-form 'tqr' IS a derivationMap key. Kept verbatim.
template column has aggregate-role-from='[State/Province]'; cosmetic secondary-role hint carried onto the target (verification-report correction: GREEN -> YELLOW).

hazards.detail: [Region] aggregate-role-from='[State/Province]' is not rewritten; target inherits the secondary-role hint. Verification report corrects this template from GREEN to YELLOW. Safe under binding, so fast_path_eligible stays true.

templates.description: An aggregated measure over an exact date with template-owned average and +/-3-sigma reference lines/bands.

slots.notes: XML instance is [md:Order Date:ok] derivation='MDY' (exact date, discrete/ordinal). 'md'/'MDY' is NOT a derivationMap key nor a §2.2 Derivation member. Normalized to the closest canonical token 'tdy' (TruncDay = day-level date). See non-canonical-template-derivation hazard for the fidelity caveat.
same [sum:Profit:qk] instance drives rows, color, and the average/stdev reference lines (template-owned analytics).

hazards.detail: Template date instance uses derivation short-form 'md' (long 'MDY', an exact discrete date), absent from templates.ts derivationMap and the §2.2 Derivation union. Manifest records the closest canonical 'tdy' (TruncDay). RESIDUAL RISK: TruncDay (continuous day) differs from MDY (discrete exact date) at runtime; the fast-path date binding may render continuous rather than discrete. XML trusted for slot/field structure; derivation token normalized.

average and sample-stdev (+/-3 factor) reference lines/bands are bound to [sum:Profit:qk] and repaired only because Profit is a bound slot; no dataset-specific calc IDs are referenced, so this is not a DATASET_SPECIFIC_FORMULA blocker.

templates.description: One bubble per detail-grain member; X and Y are two measures, bubble size is a third measure.

slots.notes: detail/grain dimension: one bubble per member.

templates.description: Two measures on a shared/combined axis over a truncated date; Measure Names colors the two series (template-owned).
avoid_when: The two measures share the same scale - use a single shared axis instead of a dual axis
Axes are not synchronized or have different zero points - unsynchronized dual axes imply a false correlation
More than two measures are involved - use separate charts
The measures are unrelated - never overlay unrelated measures on a dual axis to manufacture a pattern

slots.notes: XML instance is [tmn:Order Date:qk] derivation='Month-Trunc'. 'tmn' is the real Month-Trunc short-form live Tableau writes; the manifest emits it verbatim. templates.ts derivationMap keys both 'tmn' (canonical) and legacy 'tmo' to 'Month-Trunc'.
Tableau pseudo-field [:Measure Names]; template owns it fully. Not a <column> declaration, so it is exempt from the column-existence check. Never user-bindable.

hazards.detail: RULING (H3.2 tmn/tmo reconciliation): 'tmn' is the REAL Month-Trunc short-form live Tableau writes — confirmed in this template XML (derivation='Month-Trunc' name='[tmn:Order Date:qk]'), in real Tableau Public workbooks (data/twb-example-index.json), in src/metadata/fields.ts:434, and in tactics/tree/column-instance-prefixes.md. The binder/manifests standardize on canonical 'tmn'; templates.ts derivationMap accepts BOTH 'tmn' and legacy 'tmo' as aliases → 'Month-Trunc'. XML is ground truth; no derivation normalization is applied.
color encoding uses the [:Measure Names] pseudo-field. It is template-owned and non-bindable; PSEUDO_FIELD_REQUIRED is NOT a blocker here (readiness stays GREEN).
rows shelf is a combined-axis expression ([sum:Profit:qk] + [avg:Discount:qk]); both measure slots must bind or the combined axis dangles (both required, satisfied by coverage).

templates.description: One point per detail-grain member; X and Y are two measures; color is a template calc; a second dimension adds detail.

slots.notes: template column has aggregate-role-from='[State/Province]'; NOT rewritten by replaceFieldReferences (cosmetic secondary-role hint carried onto the target).

hazards.detail: Calc formula references bare [Profit]/[Sales]; rewriteFormulaFieldRefs repairs them ONLY because Profit & Sales are bindable bare-key slots. Gate #6 asserts both are bound.
[Region] aggregate-role-from='[State/Province]' is not rewritten; target field inherits the template's secondary-role hint (verification-report correction).

templates.description: A strip/bar-code plot: one tick per detail-grain member along an aggregated measure axis, grouped by a category on rows.

slots.notes: template aggregates Sales with Avg (not Sum): [avg:Sales:qk].
detail/grain dimension carrying semantic-role='[Country].[ISO3166_2]'; the geo semantic-role is a cosmetic hint carried onto the target.
detail/grain dimension carrying semantic-role='[State].[Name]'; cosmetic hint carried onto the target.

hazards.detail: [Country/Region] and [State/Province] carry semantic-role attributes; these are not rewritten by replaceFieldReferences and are carried onto the target fields. They define mark grain, not a geographic map. Verification report agrees the readiness is GREEN (contrast spatial-filled-map which stays YELLOW).

templates.description: A single big-number text card: one measure aggregated and shown as label text.

slots.notes: template field is the generic name 'Value'; binds a single quantitative measure. Portability is 'across the committed fixture classes + render-verified' (see portability_evidence), not a blanket 'any dataset'.

templates.description: A pie whose wedge angles are an aggregated measure, one wedge per category (color).
avoid_when: More than ~3-5 categories - humans compare angles poorly; a sorted horizontal bar reads more precisely and scales to any count
Slice sizes are close in value - the differences become impossible to judge by angle
Showing change over time - use a line or stacked area instead
Any analytical context where parts must be compared precisely - prefer a stacked bar or treemap (pie is usually the wrong choice)
Never render as a 3D pie - perspective distorts the proportions

slots.notes: template column has aggregate-role-from='[State/Province]'; cosmetic secondary-role hint carried onto the target (verification-report correction: GREEN -> YELLOW).

hazards.detail: [Region] aggregate-role-from='[State/Province]' is not rewritten; target inherits the secondary-role hint. Verification report corrects this template from GREEN to YELLOW. Safe under binding, so fast_path_eligible stays true.

hazards.detail: RULING (H3.2 tmn/tmo reconciliation): 'tmn' is the REAL Month-Trunc short-form live Tableau writes — confirmed in this template XML (derivation='Month-Trunc' name='[tmn:Order Date:qk]'), in real Tableau Public workbooks (data/twb-example-index.json: [sqlserver…].[tmn:Order Date:qk], [dataengine…].[tmn:DateTime (Days):ok]), in src/metadata/fields.ts:434, and in tactics/tree/column-instance-prefixes.md. The binder/manifests standardize on canonical 'tmn'; templates.ts derivationMap accepts BOTH 'tmn' and legacy 'tmo' as aliases → 'Month-Trunc'. XML is ground truth; no derivation normalization is applied.

templates.description: One floating (gantt) bar per category: the bar starts at a reference-line measure and its length is a template calc = parsed actual value − reference line. Deviation of an actual from a reference, per member.
avoid_when: the actual/score field is not a leading-numeric string — INT() parses only a leading integer, so compound strings like '35-31' or non-numeric text produce the wrong bar length
both endpoints are already numeric measures — bind a simpler dual-measure or reference-line deviation chart instead of the string-parse calc

slots.notes: The row/category each floating bar is drawn for (one mark per member).

The reference-line endpoint on columns AND a formula input to the size calc. Derivation 'min' is authored fidelity from the checkpoint — correct only at one-row-per-category grain (see min-derivation-per-row hazard).

Compound-score STRING parsed by INT() inside the size calc. Bindable + required so templates.ts rewriteFormulaFieldRefs repairs the bare [Actual Input] token and Gate 6 can prove the dependency is bound. Never placed on a shelf (no column-instance).

FIDELITY from the final 'format' rung (absent in rung-1 floating-bars.xml): the over/under COLOR split (teal=over/right, red=under/left). A plain categorical color column-instance [none:Segment:nk]; parameterized like any bindable dimension so replaceFieldReferences substitutes it — never hardcoded to the Super Bowl O/U Winner field. Required so computeFixtureBind proves a full 4-field bind and the color encoding is never left dangling on an unbound field.

calcs.notes: Bar length = parsed actual − reference. formula_refs are the template_field names of the two bindable dependency slots; rewriteFormulaFieldRefs repairs them on bind (same mechanism as correlation-scatter's Profit Ratio).

hazards.detail: INT([Actual Input]) parses a leading integer out of a compound score string; the parse is dataset-shape-specific (yields NULL/garbage on non-numeric or embedded-format text). Advisory only — surfaced via avoid_when and gated by render_verified, not a hard fast_path_blocker.

The size calc references bare [Actual Input]/[Reference Value]; templates.ts rewriteFormulaFieldRefs repairs them ONLY because both are bindable bare-key slots. Gate 6 asserts both are bound before injection.

The size calc and the reference line use derivation 'min' (authored fidelity from the checkpoint). Min equals the row value only when there is exactly one mark per Category; a category with multiple rows collapses to the minimum. Bind at one-row-per-category grain or override the derivation.

templates.description: Dual-mark arrow chart (Line shaft + Shape arrowhead) comparing an actual combined score to a betting/reference line per member, newest-first, with text label columns (season / id / line) and an inline O/U-diff spine. Arrows point right/teal when the actual finished OVER the line and left/red when UNDER, colored by a SIGN(actual - line) calc. Faithful compile of the Super Bowl 'O/U Line' golden worksheet.
avoid_when: the actual field is not a compound '<prefix> <winner>-<loser>' score string — the nested SPLIT/TRIM parse assumes a single-token prefix, a space, then a hyphen-separated winner-loser pair (e.g. 'PHI 40-22'); a different shape parses the wrong combined score
the row identifier is not a '<name> (<year>)' pattern — the season/id split parses the parenthetical year out of the member label

distinct teal/red over/under hue and the left/right arrowhead glyphs are required on first render — that palette+shape mapping is datasource-level style in the golden and is NOT carried by this worksheet-only template (see hazards datasource-style-dropped)

slots.notes: The compound member label '<name> (<year>)' (e.g. 'Super Bowl LIX (2024)'). SPLIT-parsed by the season and id calcs into the row header text columns. Never shelved raw.
Compound-score STRING (e.g. 'KC 25-22') SPLIT-parsed into winning + losing scores that sum to the actual combined score. Never shelved raw.

The O/U betting line. Shown as a discrete text label column on rows ([sum:Total Score Bet:ok]), as an endpoint of the arrow's shared measure axis ([sum:Total Score Bet:qk]), and as the subtrahend of the over/under sign and O/U-diff calcs.

calcs.notes: TRIM(SPLIT([Super Bowl],'(',1)) — the member name/id before the parenthetical year; feeds the SB # roman-numeral label calc.
int(TRIM(SPLIT(SPLIT([Super Bowl],'(',2),')',1)))-1 — the season year parsed from the parenthetical. The outermost rows dimension and the DESC (newest-first) sort key.

"SB "+CASE int([Super Bowl - Split 1])
    WHEN 2 THEN "II"
    WHEN 3 THEN "III"
    WHEN 4 THEN "IV"
    WHEN 5 THEN "V"
    WHEN 6 THEN "VI"
    WHEN 7 THEN "VII"

calcs.notes: 'SB '+CASE int([Super Bowl - Split 1]) WHEN ... — the roman-numeral member label text column (a template-INTERNAL calc consuming the id calc).
TRIM(SPLIT([Final],'-',1)) — the winner-side token ('KC 25') of the compound score; feeds the winning-score parse.
INT(SPLIT([Final - Split 1],' ',2)) — the winning team's points (template-INTERNAL: consumes Final - Split 1).

[Final - Split 1 - Split 2]+[Final - Split 2] — the actual combined score (winning + losing). The far endpoint of the arrow ([sum:...:qk] on cols) and a template-INTERNAL input to the sign and O/U-diff calcs.

sign(([Calculation_3985421010944000])-([Total Score Bet])) — +1 over / -1 under. Drives BOTH the arrow color and the arrowhead shape encoding. NOTE: the discrete teal/red hue and left/right-triangle glyph MAP is datasource-level style in the golden (dropped by extractWorksheet); this calc only supplies the driving field.
([Calculation_3985421010944000])-([Total Score Bet]) — the signed variance from the line. Drives the inline O/U-diff spine (pane 6 Bar + pane 7 Circle) and its +/-43 reference band.

hazards.detail: CARRIED (fidelity fix): the golden's datasource-level <style-rule element='mark'> value→hue (color palette) + value→glyph (shape) maps (2 color + 2 shape) AND the 1 datasource-scope column-instance declaration(s) they require are captured into manifest.datasource_style and re-spliced on apply (evals/lib/tier1-fastpath.mjs spliceDatasourceStyle) at the proven anchors — column-instance before <layout>, <style> before <semantic-values> — with calc refs namespaced per-apply identically to the worksheet fragment. Previously dropped by extractWorksheet (worksheet-only). Original loss detail: The golden's discrete over/under color palette (teal #76b7b2 over, red #e15759 under) AND the arrowhead shape mapping (:filled/right-triangle over, :filled/left-triangle under) are declared in the DATASOURCE-level <style><style-rule element='mark'> block (golden twb lines 441-460), NOT inside the <worksheet>. scripts/compile-checkpoint-template.mjs extractWorksheet extracts only the <worksheet> element, so this value->hue / value->glyph map is dropped at compile time. The template carries the color/shape ENCODINGS (which field drives them) but the marks fall back to the default palette + default shape on apply. Faithful hue+glyph requires re-injecting the datasource-level mark style; not expressible in the worksheet-only slot-spec.

The actual-score and season/id calcs parse combined tokens out of compound strings via nested SPLIT/TRIM (golden semantics): 'Final' as '<prefix> <winner>-<loser>' and 'Super Bowl' as '<name> (<year>)'. Dataset-shape-specific — a different string shape yields NULL/garbage. Advisory (surfaced via avoid_when + render_verified gate), not a hard fast_path_blocker.

Calcs reference bare bindable [Super Bowl]/[Final]/[Total Score Bet]; templates.ts rewriteFormulaFieldRefs repairs them on bind because each is a bindable bare-key slot. Template-internal calc-to-calc refs (e.g. [Calculation_3985421010944000]) are intentionally left unmapped.

datasource_style.style_rule:
<style-rule element='mark'>
          <encoding attr='color' field='[sum:Calculation_3985421012422657:ok]' type='palette'>
            <map to='#76b7b2'>
              <bucket>1</bucket>
            </map>
            <map to='#e15759'>
              <bucket>-1</bucket>
            </map>
          </encoding>
          <encoding attr='shape' field='[sum:Calculation_3985421012422657:ok]' type='shape'>
            <map to=':filled/left-triangle'>
              <bucket>-1</bucket>
            </map>
            <map to=':filled/right-triangle'>
              <bucket>1</bucket>
            </map>
          </encoding>
        </style-rule>

templates.description: O/U-difference spine chart: one gray Bar-from-zero + Circle-dot lollipop per member showing the signed variance of the actual combined score from the betting/reference line, newest-first, with a +/-43 reference band and the same season/id/line label columns. The companion 'diff' view to the ww-ou-arrow chart, isolated from the Super Bowl 'O/U Line' golden worksheet.
avoid_when: the actual field is not a compound '<prefix> <winner>-<loser>' score string — the nested SPLIT/TRIM parse assumes a single-token prefix, a space, then a hyphen-separated winner-loser pair (e.g. 'PHI 40-22')
the member label is not a '<name> (<year>)' pattern — the season/id split parses the parenthetical year out of it
you want the over/under direction encoded by hue on this view — the spine marks are intentionally gray; over/under teal/red belongs to the arrow chart

slots.notes: Compound member label '<name> (<year>)'. SPLIT-parsed by the season and id calcs into the row header text columns. Never shelved raw.
Compound-score STRING SPLIT-parsed into winning + losing scores that sum to the actual combined score feeding the O/U-diff. Never shelved raw.
The O/U betting line. Shown as a discrete text label column on rows ([sum:Total Score Bet:ok]) and used as the subtrahend of the O/U-diff calc.

calcs.notes: TRIM(SPLIT([Super Bowl],'(',1)) — member name/id before the parenthetical year; feeds the SB # roman-numeral label calc.
int(TRIM(SPLIT(SPLIT([Super Bowl],'(',2),')',1)))-1 — the season year. Outermost rows dimension and the DESC (newest-first) sort key.

hazards.detail: The golden has ONE combined worksheet; this diff-only view is a documented derivation of it (cols restricted to the O/U-diff spine, arrow panes 3/4/5 removed, +/-43 reference lines relocated into the Bar pane). It could NOT be live-render-verified in this environment (no MCP apply tools), so render_verified stays 'none'. Renderability of the isolated spine is a residual risk until a live golden-match.
